markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Test interrupted indexing, interrupted editor save, lost agent, stale lease, partial validation, and restart after crash. This MT belongs to ParallelSwarmStateRecovery. Make WP-009 safe for local/cloud swarm work, backend navigation, and repeated session recovery.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Local and cloud swarm lanes must be observable, attributable, bounded, recoverable, and safe for parallel agents.
Every MT must leave enough receipts, state markers, and next-step context for session-limit recovery by a no-context model.
scope.expected_behavior: StateRecoveryNegativeCases leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: StateRecoveryNegativeCases is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence.summary: MT-223 hardens negative recovery paths across interrupted quiet indexing, post-KIR quiet receipt failure, stale indexing lease queue fairness, interrupted rich-document save recovery, partial validation progress handoff, missing/corrupt checkpoint false receipts, and restart-after-crash Postgres state reconstruction. The quiet indexing start path now writes the indexing lease, KIR start event/run row, and quiet background receipt in one Postgres transaction so failure rolls back all receipt surfaces.

commands.command: cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --test parallel_swarm_state_recovery_tests mt223_ -- --nocapture
cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --test parallel_swarm_state_recovery_tests quiet_ -- --nocapture
commands.result: PASS; 5/5 quiet recovery regression tests passed
PASS; non-quiet code-index caller impact smoke test passed
PASS; CRLF notices only, including foreign dirty visual-debugger files

evidence.summary: Reviewer confirmed Harvey's prior orphan window is closed: KIR start receipt/run row, indexing lease, and quiet receipt now share one transaction in start_quiet_run. They found no blocking issues; residual uncertainty is limited to an availability-only stale-lease expiry race that fails closed without orphaning side effects.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-223 hardens seven parallel-swarm state-recovery negative cases (interrupted indexing start, quiet receipt failure rollback, stale-lease reclaim/queue fairness, interrupted editor-save reclaim, partial-validation handoff, missing/corrupt checkpoint false-receipt suppression, and crash-restart reconstruction). The implementation makes the quiet-indexing start path atomic across lease + KIR run row + start event + quiet receipt in one PostgreSQL transaction with rollback, and fixes lease-queue fairness so a queued writer is not leapfrogged. All proof runs against real Handshake-native PostgreSQL/EventLedger with isolated schemas and the full migration chain; no SQLite, in-memory, mock-only, or placeholder paths.
findings: engine.rs start_quiet_run (commit ab3a5053): the quiet-indexing start path now opens one tx (self.db.pool().begin()), acquires the indexing lease via try_acquire_indexing_lease_tx, writes the KIR start event + run row via start_run_with_id_tx, and records the quiet receipt via record_quiet_background_work_tx, committing only on full success and rolling back (tx.rollback()) on any failure. This closes the prior orphan window where lease/KIR/quiet receipts could be written independently.

state_recovery.rs: added tx-scoped variants (try_acquire_indexing_lease_tx, insert_indexing_lease_outcome_tx, record_quiet_background_work_tx, active/queued_index_writer_for_scope_tx) by threading &mut Transaction<Postgres> through; non-tx wrappers retained for callers. Queue-fairness bug fixed: enqueue/acquire now consult queued_index_writer_for_scope so a new request does not jump ahead of an already-queued writer (lines ~3071-3130, 3303-3375).

postgres.rs: append_kernel_event_with_executor made pub(crate) so the engine can append the KIR start event inside the shared transaction executor — a real EventLedger write on the same tx, not a separate connection.

Test proof is real PostgreSQL: tests/knowledge_pg_support.rs knowledge_pg() requires a base DB URL, creates an isolated schema, installs pgcrypto + digest shims, and runs the full migration chain; recovery_store() (test file line 174) connects a real PgPool to that schema. All MT-223 tests skip (return) when no PG is present rather than falling back to a mock.

mt223_quiet_receipt_failure_rolls_back_index_run_and_lease (line 4483) injects a real plpgsql BEFORE-INSERT trigger that raises on the quiet-work table (install_quiet_indexing_receipt_failure, line 745) and then asserts COUNT(*)=0 across knowledge_index_runs, knowledge_parallel_indexing_lease_queue, knowledge_agent_quiet_background_work, and kernel_event_ledger for both knowledge_code_index and parallel_swarm aggregates — a genuine atomic-rollback proof against real tables.

mt223_stale_indexing_lease_reclaim_then_queued_writer_is_promotable (4603) and mt223_stale_indexing_lease_enqueue_does_not_leapfrog_queued_writer (4688) force lease expiry via UPDATE ... expires_at_utc = NOW() - INTERVAL and verify reclaim + correct queue promotion with a fresh acquired EventLedger receipt (KNOWLEDGE_INDEX_RUN_STARTED / status acquired).

mt223_restart_after_crash_reconstructs_swarm_state_from_postgres (5044) seeds claim/handoff/checkpoint/lease/quiet rows, drops the store (simulated crash), builds a fresh store on the same pool, and proves inspect_swarm_evidence, recover_from_checkpoint, and project_swarm_dashboard all reconstruct the persisted state from PostgreSQL.

mt223_missing_checkpoint_recovery_does_not_emit_receipt (4264) and the corrupt-payload-hash test (4303) prove no false recovery receipt or EventLedger event is written on bad checkpoints; mt223_partial_validation_progress_handoff_is_not_reported_as_pass (4964) proves a progress handoff is not counted as pass in the dashboard projection.

No deferred-live escapes: grep of engine.rs and state_recovery.rs for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/*Unavailable/not yet wired/pending MT-/placeholder returned zero hits. No SQLite/in-memory/mock strings in the test file.

Aside (non-blocking, per ground rules): lifecycle.claimed_by is null while completed_by is set; status is READY_FOR_VALIDATION (not self-signed COMPLETED), so sub-rule-3 is satisfied. Standard winding-down paperwork state, not a code defect.
evidence_checked: git show ab3a5053 (engine.rs, state_recovery.rs, postgres.rs diff)
grep for deferred-live escapes / SQLite / mock (zero hits)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
